q: In a vernier callipers, 50 vernier scale divisions are equal to 48 main scale divisions. If one main scale$division=0.05 mm , then the least count of the vernier callipers is _ _ _ _ mm.
explanation: Value of one Main Scale Division, 1$MSD=0.05 mm Number of Vernier Scale Divisions$m=50 Number of Main Scale Divisions is n=48 50 VSD=48 MSD ⇒ 1 VSD=48 50 MSD The Least Count of a Vernier instrument is the smallest measurement that can be taken accurately$. Least Count (LC) of Vernier$scale=1 MSD-1 VSD Using the least count formula, LC=1 MSD-1 VSD ⇒ LC=1 MSD-( 48 50 ) MSD ⇒ LC=( 1-48 50 ) MSD ⇒ LC=( 50-48 50 ) MSD ⇒ LC=2 50 MSD=2 50 \times  0.05mm=0.002 mm Therefore, the smallest measurement that this Vernier calliper can record is 0.002 mm . Hence, the correct option is (A).
